content:
✅ Approved the contribution.
Comment: I enjoyed this clash! Not too difficult, and good for clash of code. Good job!

✅ Approved the contribution.
Comment: Cool puzzle

✅ Approved the contribution.
Comment: Fun puzzle, which is also not too easy and not too hard.

Goal
* Please add the following after the second paragraph:

The two operations may be the same or different.

For example, if the numbers are 89, 64 and 10, some of the possibilities are:
• (89 + 64) + 10 = 163
• (89 + 64) * 10 = 1530
• (89 - 64) * 10 = 250
• (89 * 64) - 10 = 5686

Constraints
* Please add a constraint for [[num]]:
1 ≤ each [[num]] ≤ 1000

Stub generator
* Please add a write statement, e.g. write answer.

Cases
* Please remove the trailing newlines from the output of the cases below:
  • Complex One (Test 4)
  • Validator 4

* Please add a pair of cases where the first two [[num]]s are 2.

* Please add extra cases to cover the following operation sequences:
  • + followed by - (validator)
  • - followed by + (test)
  • - followed by - (test & validator)
  • - followed by * (test & validator)

Thanks.
Very good cases. I don't have further comments at the moment. 🙂
